title: phaseolus vulgaris var. atrovariegatus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. atrovariegatus is a variety of the common bean plant, specifically a type of black bean known for its unique atrovariegatus or "black and white" variegated foliage. The medicinal properties of Phaseolus vulgaris var. atrovariegatus are said to include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Consuming Phaseolus vulgaris var. atrovariegatus has been reported to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, regulating blood sugar levels, and providing a rich source of essential nutrients such as protein, fiber, and minerals. In traditional herbal medicine, Phaseolus vulgaris var. atrovariegatus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in the form of capsules, powders, or tinctures.
